species: ANME-2c archaeon in syntrophic consortium with sulfate-reducing bacteria (marine anaerobic methanotrophic archaea)

growth_records.strain: Amon Mud Volcano marine enrichment (NAUTINIL 2003 sample); not axenic, mixed archaeal/bacterial consortium
growth_records.medium: Synthetic marine sulfate-reducer medium, pH 7.4, methane headspace
growth_records.cite: Müller et al. Atomic resolution structures of the methane-activating enzyme in anaerobic methanotrophy reveal extensive post-translational modifications. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-63387-1. [PMC12413448]
growth_records.snippet: sediment slurries were set up with a synthetic marine sulfate reducer medium at pH 7.4 and methane headspace at 20 °C... The activity-based doubling time for both cultures, monitored by sulfide measurements, was about 4
growth_records.notes: Paper directly reports (Methods, 'ANME-2c cultivation') an activity-based doubling time of 'about 4 to 6 months' for methane-oxidizing/sulfate-reducing enrichment cultures grown on methane (electron donor) + sulfate (electron acceptor) in synthetic marine sulfate-reducer medium at pH 7.4, 20 degC, monitored via sulfide production. Doubling_time_h and growth_rate_per_h fields here use the midpoint of the reported range (5 months = 3600 h): mu = ln2/3600 = 0.0001925 h^-1. Range bounds: 4 months (2880 h) -> mu=0.000241 h^-1; 6 months (4320 h) -> mu=0.0001605 h^-1. This is a mixed archaeal-bacterial syntrophic consortium, not an axenic culture; rate reflects population-level (not single-species) activity.
